summary: Russian forces reported the liberation of Dobropasovo in Dnipropetrovsk region. Ukrainian attacks caused civilian injuries in Ryazan, Pavlograd, and Druzhkivka. Both sides continue intensive UAV and guided bomb operations across multiple fronts.
key_points: Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of Dobropasovo village near Pokrovskoe in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Two persons injured by a Ukrainian attack on Ryazan on 15 May remain hospitalized (Source: @tass_agency/377540).
Ukrainian forces reportedly drove Russian troops out of Vozdvizhevka in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @tass_agency/377537).
SBU detained a Russian spotter codenamed “Shaman” in Lyman after a firefight that resulted in his death (Source: @UkraineNow/81445).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/62777).
Multiple reactive and reconnaissance UAVs were observed over Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Kherson regions (Source: @eRadarrua/93685, @eRadarrua/93676, @kpszsu/62766).
SBU detained a Russian agent alleged to have coordinated the May 24 attack on Kyiv (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30426).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces announced the liberation of Dobropasovo in Dnipropetrovsk region. Ukrainian attacks injured civilians in Ryazan, Pavlograd, and Druzhkivka, while both sides intensified UAV and guided bomb operations across several fronts. SBU operations resulted in the detention of Russian operatives linked to reconnaissance and the Kyiv attack.

points: Ukrainian forces reportedly pushed Russian troops out of Vozdvizhevka in Zaporizhzhia, indicating a localized counter‑offensive (Source: @tass_agency/377537).
A Ukrainian attack on Ryazan on 15 May injured two individuals who remain in hospital, showing cross‑border strike capability (Source: @tass_agency/377540).
In Pavlograd, a strike hit a multi‑storey building, hospitalising three men aged 18, 43, and 45 with moderate injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81443).
SBU detained a Russian spotter “Shaman” in Lyman after he attempted to reconnoitre Ukrainian positions; he was killed during the operation (Source: @UkraineNow/81445).

Ukrainian Defense Ministry advisor reported Russian strikes on the Ukrainian Ground Forces command building and the Lukyanovka (Artem) missile plant, though the command building had not been used since the war began (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30410).
The Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of the village Dobropasovo near Pokrovskoe in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aviation bomb launches on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, indicating continued air‑strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62777).
A Russian‑linked agency claimed a downed gas tanker “Arktik Metagaz” was damaged near Malta while en route from Murmansk to China, accusing Ukraine of the attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81452).
Russian forces reportedly used a reactive UAV over Ripky in Chernihiv region and over several locations in Sumy, demonstrating expanded UAV deployment (Source: @eRadarrua/93685).
A threat of ballistic weapon use from the south was announced but later reported as cancelled, reflecting fluctuating strategic posturing (Source: @kpszsu/62758, @kpszsu/62735).
Ukrainian forces claim to have driven Russian troops out of Vozdvizhevka in Zaporizhzhia, a key settlement on the eastern front (Source: @tass_agency/377537).
Russian guided aviation bombs were launched on Zaporizhzhia region, targeting eastern positions (Source: @kpszsu/62777).
Multiple UAVs were observed over eastern Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Shakhtarske, indicating reconnaissance activity (Source: @kpszsu/62776).
A reactive UAV was reported over Ripky (Chernihiv) and Sumy areas, suggesting cross‑border UAV incursions on the eastern axis (Source: @eRadarrua/93685).
Ukrainian SBU detained a Russian spotter “Shaman” in Lyman after a firefight, highlighting intelligence battles in the east (Source: @UkraineNow/81445).
UAV activity was reported over Chernihiv region, including a reactive UAV near Gorodny and repeated routes near Slavutych along the Dnipro (Source: @eRadarrua/93676, @eRadarrua/93696).
Ukrainian reconnaissance noted UAVs moving north‑west over Kharkiv region, passing Kovyagi (Source: @eRadarrua/93676).
Multiple UAV groups were observed heading toward Kharkiv city from various directions, including south‑east and south‑west courses (Source: @kpszsu/62755).
A UAV was sighted over Kherson region on a north‑west trajectory, indicating northern front activity (Source: @kpszsu/62766).
Russian forces launched guided aviation bombs on Donetsk region, affecting northern sectors of the front (Source: @kpszsu/62777).
Russian guided aviation bombs were also deployed on Donetsk region, showing southern front air activity (Source: @kpszsu/62777).
A Russian threat of ballistic weapons from the south was announced, though later cancelled (Source: @kpszsu/62758, @kpszsu/62735).
Ukrainian attack on Druzhkivka in Donetsk region involved a FAB strike on a multi‑storey building, killing a 70‑year‑old woman and injuring several others (Source: @UkraineNow/81440).
Ukrainian forces reported a strike on a multi‑storey building in Pavlograd, a city in the southern Dnipropetrovsk area (Source: @UkraineNow/81443).
Ukrainian SBU detained a Russian agent linked to the May 24 Kyiv attack, indicating southern‑origin intelligence operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30426).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, demonstrating continued air strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62777).
Ukrainian attacks reached Ryazan, injuring two civilians, showing capability to strike deep into Russian airspace (Source: @tass_agency/377540).
Multiple reactive UAVs were observed over Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Kherson, indicating widespread UAV deployment by Russian forces (Source: @eRadarrua/93685, @eRadarrua/93676, @kpszsu/62766).
UAV reconnaissance flights were reported over Zhytomyr and Dnipropetrovsk, following routes near Slavutych along the Dnipro River (Source: @eRadarrua/93696).
A threat of ballistic weapon use from the south was announced but later cancelled, reflecting fluctuating strategic air‑defense postures (Source: @kpszsu/62758, @kpszsu/62735).

key_developments: Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of Dobropasovo village near Pokrovskoe in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30415).
Two civilians injured by a Ukrainian strike on Ryazan on 15 May remain hospitalized (Source: @tass_agency/377540).
Ukrainian forces reportedly expelled Russian troops from Vozdvizhevka in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @tass_agency/377537).
SBU detained a Russian spotter codenamed “Shaman” in Lyman after a firefight that resulted in his death (Source: @UkraineNow/81445).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/62777).
Multiple reactive UAVs were observed over Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Kherson, indicating expanded Russian UAV operations (Source: @eRadarrua/93685, @eRadarrua/93676, @kpszsu/62766).
SBU detained a Russian agent alleged to have coordinated the May 24 attack on Kyiv (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30426).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces continue to leverage UAVs and guided aviation bombs across the eastern, northern, and southern fronts, maintaining pressure on Ukrainian positions. Ukraine demonstrates capability to strike deep into Russian territory, as seen in Ryazan, and to conduct counter‑offensives such as the reported recapture of Vozdvizhevka. Intelligence operations remain intense, with SBU detaining multiple Russian operatives linked to reconnaissance and the Kyiv attack, indicating a high level of covert activity.
sections.regional_response: Western intelligence outlets report increased scrutiny of Russian UAV proliferation, while Ukrainian authorities emphasize the need for improved air defenses. Local administrations in affected Russian regions, such as Ryazan, have reported civilian casualties, potentially influencing domestic sentiment. The detention of Russian agents by SBU may lead to heightened security measures in border and urban areas.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV flight paths suggest a focus on reconnaissance of logistical hubs near Slavutych, Kharkiv, and the Dnipro corridor. The cancellation of the ballistic threat indicates possible diplomatic or technical constraints on Russia's strategic weapons use. Continued SBU arrests of Russian operatives highlight effective counter‑intelligence efforts, but also reveal ongoing infiltration attempts.
